title: Webera coriacea
intro: Webera coriacea, also known as Himalayan Webera or Coriaceous Webera, is a species of flowering plant in the family Acanthaceae. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant activities, making it beneficial for the treatment of various health issues such as fever, respiratory problems, and digestive disorders. Bioactive constituents of Webera coriacea include alkaloids, glycosides, and flavonoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The plant is commonly used in traditional medicine and is prepared into herbal teas, decoctions, and infusions, as well as being incorporated into topical creams and ointments for skin and wound care applications.
taxonomy: Webera coriacea is a species of flowering plant in the family Acanthaceae. This plant is part of the genus Webera which consists of various species of flowering plants. Webera coriacea is native to the tropical regions of Asia, where it is commonly found growing in humid environments. The classification of Webera coriacea is further divided into subcategories, with its binomial nomenclature providing a unique identification. The classification of Webera coriacea is still being studied and documented by botanists and scientists.
